subject: input: support for mouseX and mice in EVIOCGRAB mode.
author: Sorgelig

upstream.vanilla_quote: drivers/input/input.c:120-124: grab delivers exclusively to grabbing handle, no second pass. drivers/input/mousedev.c:773-782: mousedev_fops has no .unlocked_ioctl (EVIOCGRAB returns -ENOTTY)

impact.effect_if_absent: USB mouse pointer stops working the moment a core launches or OSD is visible; Main_MiSTer grabs the evdev node for exclusive input handling, which under vanilla 6.18.38 silences ALL other handlers on the device, including mousedev. Without this patch, mousedev gets no events and the pointer freezes.
affected_hardware: any USB mouse connected to MiSTer

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: input.cpp:5177 (opens both event* and mouse* nodes), input.cpp:5237 (writes ImPS/2 magic to mouse node), input.cpp:6144 (reads 4-byte PS/2 packets from mouse), input.cpp:6463 (grabs every fd including mouse node via EVIOCGRAB)

forward_port.conflicts: input_to_handler() replaced by per-handle ->handle_events() method (upstream d469647bafd9, v6.11); RCU read section converted to scoped_guard(rcu) (upstream 21d8dd0daf4c, v6.14); struct input_handler already has passive_observer field, so new flag named ignore_grab to avoid confusion

superseded_by: 52a56ae3dc7f82ff6339c1be1de05d8b2d997ef9 (tap-to-click suppression for DS4/DualSense, merged into same patch)

notes: LOAD-BEARING PATCH: Without this, every MiSTer user with a USB mouse experiences complete pointer loss when launching any core. The original commit (2019) is from the fork and not upstream; vanilla 6.18.38 still delivers grabbed device events exclusively to the grabbing handler, starving mousedev entirely. Carried in patch 0026 with a cleaner re-implementation using an explicit input_handler->ignore_grab opt-in flag instead of the original's string-matching on handle->name. The forward-ported patch handles upstream structural changes: (1) input_to_handler() no longer exists, replaced by per-handle ->handle_events() method (Torokhov d469647bafd9, v6.11; fixed in 071b24b54d2d, v6.12); (2) RCU read section converted to scoped_guard(rcu) (21d8dd0daf4c, v6.14) so grab path exits with break not explicit unlock+return; (3) mousedev.c ioctl and RCU dereference rewritten to match evdev's idiom with rcu_dereference_protected()+lockdep_is_held(). Third change in patch 0026 (tap-to-click suppression for DS4/DualSense controller touchpads) was merged from commit 52a56ae3d. Provenance doc explicitly covers this as class D constraint A12 (Main_MiSTer relies on mousedev receiving events while evdev is EVIOCGRAB-ed).
